idea.title: Tier-1 Localiser
idea.hook: Translate incoming hooks across 5 region columns by spending limited glyph tokens before the queue overflows.
idea.hook_ru: Локализуй приходящие хуки в 5 региональных колонках, тратя ограниченные глифы, пока очередь не переполнится.
idea.prototype: 5-column board (US/EU/JP/KR/CN). Each turn a 'hook card' (icon + 3 keyword slots) drops into one column. Player has a hand of glyph tokens (color-coded: trust, hype, clarity, status, value). Drag tokens onto a card's slots to match that region's preferred mix (shown as a 3-color recipe at the column top). Filled-correctly cards leave board + add score + refill some tokens; mis-filled cards stay and rot into 'churned' tiles that block the slot. Lose when any column has 4 stuck cards.
idea.mutation_of: Theme adjacency to 55_tier1_hook (also Tier-1 global-market signal cluster), but mechanic is fully distinct: column-token-recipe matching with churn-overflow lives, not asset-drag-to-store-page.
idea.why_novel: Drag-and-drop column-recipe matching with churn-as-permanent-failure is a fresh mechanic in the casual HTML5 space — combines bag-management, colour-recipe puzzle, and lane-pressure (5 columns each with their own life counter) in a way the shipped tier1_hook draft mechanic never approaches.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Token scarcity (HAND_SIZE = 6) + column-overflow lives (MAX_CHURN_PER_COL = 4 per column).
idea.lose_condition: Any single region column accumulates 4 unresolved churned cards (columnChurn[i] >= MAX_CHURN_PER_COL).

sources.title: Tier-1 Localiser
sources.snippet: Build Today #1 — Tier-1 Localiser: 5-column board, hook cards drop into columns, hand of glyph tokens, recipe match at column top. Direct synthesis of the naavik_co Eastern playbook signal + the 'tier-1 hook' must-think-globally trend.

sources.title: The Eastern Playbook for Dominating Western Audiences
sources.snippet: Tier-1 thinking signal: hooks, title, screenshots, first 20 seconds should work globally — every prototype should write an English title, one-line hook, and three screenshot captions before coding too much.

sources.title: Tier-1 prototype thinking
sources.snippet: Market/monetization signal on Tier-1 thinking influencing prototypes early. Listed in digest as a supporting source for the Localiser idea.

sources.title: Tier-1 prototype thinking (continued)
sources.snippet: Companion post in the Tier-1 thinking cluster; supports the Localiser concept of multi-region taste packaging.

synthesizer_brief: Single-screen drag-and-drop puzzle. Five vertical region columns (US/EU/JP/KR/CN) each show a fixed 3-glyph recipe at the top. Hook cards drop into columns on a tick. Player drags glyphs from a 6-token hand at the bottom onto card slots; auto-check on all 3 slots filled. >=2 correct = match (card vanishes, score, combo, refill 3 glyphs). <2 correct = churn (card stays, column churn counter +1). Lose when any column hits 4 churns. Endless score-attack with drop interval tightening every 60 points. Distinct from sibling 55_tier1_hook (which is a 4-pick store-page draft, not column-token matching).

alternatives.title: Ice Chunk Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap to fracture an icefield along cleavage lines so falling supply crates land in open water — without splitting workers off from the mainland.
alternatives.mutation_of: procedural ice destruction as supply-routing puzzle
alternatives.why_novel: Procedural ice destruction reframed as a placement/routing puzzle rather than destruction-for-its-own-sake; risk of portfolio overlap (Ice Sweeper/Frost Crack siblings).

alternatives.title: Cheap Agent Bench
alternatives.hook: Route work-orders to an expensive judgement-LLM lane or a fleet of cheap automation agents — running out of budget or quality kills your studio.
alternatives.why_novel: Dual-resource scarcity (credits + quality) over routing decisions; distinct from existing Token Budget/Throttle/Triage games (those were token-stream metaphors, not fleet management).

alternatives.title: Tiny Markov Forge
alternatives.hook: A pocket Markov chain rolls procedural weapon parts each run — pick three from a streaming bag to assemble a build that survives a 5-wave gauntlet.
alternatives.why_novel: Sub-1MB ML in a hypercasual; pick budget (3 of 9) + pass tokens (2 max) + HP across waves; from IDEAS.md (Tim pre-approved).

alternatives.title: Shader Sell Job
alternatives.hook: Layer screen-space VFX on a plain prototype to hit a target feel score before the publisher meeting — but each effect costs frame-budget you can't overspend.
alternatives.why_novel: Turn-based VFX-card placement against feel-score + frame-budget caps; distinct from existing Shader Polish Sprint action runner.
